settingsPage.title: Settings
settingsPage.dcsPathDesc: Path to your local DCS World installation. Required to automatically update sounds lists and find mods.
settingsPage.autoScanDesc: Automatically scans your DCS installation and updates the internal library on startup.
settingsPage.autoScanKeepMods: Keep imported mod assets in Library
settingsPage.autoScanKeepModsDesc: If enabled, manually imported mod assets (.zip) will not be cleared when scanning your DCS directory.
settingsPage.playWithSdefConfigDesc: Applies the gain, pitch, and loop mode from the current SDEF when previewing a sound in the Projects page.

modals.ruleWarning: Custom rules are evaluated before default rules, so they take priority.
modals.deleteTypeDesc: Are you sure you want to delete the custom type "{id}"? Any rules using this type will also be removed.
modals.importDesc1: This preset includes {types} custom sound types and {rules} matching rules.
modals.importDesc2: Do you want to import them into your local sound type database?

themePage.enableDesc: When enabled, the mod will include a Theme/ directory with your custom images and the entry.lua will mount theme textures.
themePage.disabledText: Enable the theme toggle above to configure custom DCS UI images.

tutorial.sdeflist: The full index of sound events is located in your DCS folder at \\DCS World\\Doc\\Sounds\\sdef_and_wave_list.txt. Open the SDEF List tab to learn more.
tutorial.settings: Configure your DCS paths, enable Auto-Scanner to stay up to date, and tweak your application preferences.
tutorial.library: This is where you browse and select DCS sound assets. The new Scanner will auto-detect your DCS and CoreMods to build a complete list of sounds!
tutorial.project: Here you review your selected assets. Tip: the folder highlighted in blue with a glow is the 'Leaf Folder' — the direct parent of the files you are currently seeing. It helps you stay oriented in deep structures!
tutorial.sdef: Each sound asset needs an .sdef file. The visual editor lets you tweak volume, looping, distance, pitch and more — no code needed!
tutorial.theme: Customise the look of the app. Change the background image and accent colors to make it feel like yours.
tutorial.presets: Save and load your asset selections as presets. Great for reusing selections of sounds across different mods.
tutorial.soundTypes: Manage how sounds are categorized with Custom Sound Types. You can set Regex rules to auto-assign custom icons based on the sound's file path!
tutorial.collab: Connect to a GitHub repo to sync your project with teammates. Push your mod build, leave notes, and restore earlier versions.
tutorial.build: When you're ready, hit Build to generate the complete mod folder — entry.lua, .sdef files, and your audio — ready to drop into DCS Mods.
tutorial.docs: Everything you need to know about DCS sound modding: file formats, parameters, tips and examples.
tutorial.credits: Info about the app, the developer, and useful community links. You're all set — happy modding! 🎧

defaults.title: Recommended Defaults per Sound Type
defaults.desc: The mod creator auto-fills these values based on sound type:
defaults.note: Note: A "—" means the default of the engine is used.

install.title: Installation & Troubleshooting
install.step1: Navigate to your DCS Saved Games folder.
install.step2: Build your mod in Better ModMaker choosing the destination folder above.
install.step3: Ensure your mod structure follows the standard (Sounds/Effects and Sounds/sdef).
install.step4: Launch DCS and verify the mod appears in the 'Mods' section of the settings.
install.tip1: If the resource folder doesn't exist, create it.
install.variantWarning: DCS Variant Suffixes: If you use DCS Open Beta, your saved games folder may be DCS.openbeta instead of DCS.
install.trouble1: Sounds not playing: Check that your SDEF wave paths match the actual file locations in Sounds/Effects/.
install.trouble2: Default sounds still play: Make sure mount_vfs_sound_path is present in entry.lua and the SDEF path matches original exactly.
install.trouble3: Theme not showing: Verify the Skins section exists in entry.lua.
install.trouble4: Mod not detected: Make sure the mod is in the correct Mods/resource/ directory.

audio.title: 🎵 Audio Guide for DCS Sound Modding

monoStereo.title: Mono vs Stereo
monoStereo.tip: Rule of thumb: If DCS needs to position the sound in 3D space (engines, switches, weapons), use Mono. Stereo is primarily for ambient/atmospheric sounds and UI effects.

loopOneshot.title: Loop vs One-Shot
loopOneshot.desc: DCS distinguishes between two playback modes:
loopOneshot.loop: Loop: Sound plays continuously until stopped (engines, APU, wind, avionics hum). These need seamless loop points.
loopOneshot.oneshot: One-shot: Sound plays once and stops (button clicks, switch toggles, explosions, weapon launches). Set detached = true in the SDEF.
loopOneshot.warning: Important: Looping sounds should have clean loop points — avoid clicks or pops at the start/end boundaries. Use a DAW to crossfade the loop points.

whatIs.title: What is a .sdef file?
whatIs.p1: A .sdef (Source DEFinition) file tells DCS how to play a sound. It defines which audio file to use, its volume, spatial properties, playback mode, and more.
whatIs.p2: SDEF files live in Sounds/sdef/ and reference audio files in Sounds/Effects/.

detailed.title: Detailed Parameters Explanation
detailed.listmodeDesc: Instead of playing a single file, you can provide a list of multiple `wave` files. DCS will pick one to avoid repetition. The `listmode` parameter determines how DCS picks :
detailed.listmodeRandom: RANDOM: DCS picks a random sound from the list. Best for explosions, voices, gunshots.
detailed.listmodeSeq: SEQ (Sequential): Plays the sounds in the order they are listed.
detailed.listmodeASR: ASR: Attack-Sustain-Release. Plays the 1st file on start, loops the 2nd file, and plays the 3rd file on stop.
detailed.dopplerDesc: The Doppler effect is the change in frequency (pitch) when an object approaches and then moves away from you (like an ambulance siren).
detailed.dopplerTrue: nodoppler = false (Default if not specified): DCS automatically calculates pitch distortion based on the aircraft/missile speed. Use for external physical objects (engines, missiles, flybys).
detailed.dopplerFalse: nodoppler = true: Disables the artificial Doppler effect. Essential for internal cockpit sounds (since you move at the same speed as the plane) and UI sounds.
detailed.coneDesc: By default, a sound radiates in all directions (sphere). With `direction` and cones (`cone_inner_angle`, `cone_outer_angle`), you can shape the sound to only project in a specific angle, like a jet exhaust pointing backward.

theme.title: 🎨 Theme Customization Guide

whatIs.title: What is a DCS Theme?
whatIs.desc: A theme allows you to customize the look of DCS World's user interface — including the main menu background, loading screen, briefing map, and mod icon.

fileRequirements.title: File Requirements
fileRequirements.iconUsage: Mod icon in the DCS module manager
fileRequirements.logoUsage: Logo displayed on the main menu screen
fileRequirements.loadingUsage: Background image shown during mission loading
fileRequirements.briefingUsage: Background of the mission briefing map screen

formatRecommendations.title: Format Recommendations
formatRecommendations.resolution: Use the exact recommended resolutions for best results
formatRecommendations.hd: Loading and menu backgrounds should be 1920×1080 minimum but can be larger (variable)
formatRecommendations.vram: Larger images may work but will use more VRAM

theme.tip: Theme support must be enabled in the entry.lua with a Skins section and mount_vfs_texture_path. The Build page handles this automatically when you enable Theme.

gain.title: Gain-Distance Model
gain.desc: DCS uses a multi-stage gain curve based on distance. This visual model shows how volume (gain) changes as you move away.

rampUp.title: 1. Ramp Up
rampUp.desc: From silent_radius to peak_radius. Sound fades in linearly from 0 to 100%.

peak.title: 2. Peak Zone
peak.desc: At peak_radius, the volume is at its maximum (Gain = 1.0).

reference.title: 3. Reference
reference.desc: At inner_radius, volume is defined as 0.85 of max. This is the standard DCS reference point.

falloff.title: 4. Falloff
falloff.desc: From inner_radius to outer_radius, volume drops following an inverse curve to 0.

f18.title: F/A-18C Sound Reference
f18.desc: Detailed guide for Hornet sound modding. Reference these paths and settings for your custom sounds.
f18.tipMono: Engines & Cockpit: Most exterior sounds and interior switches are Mono for 3D positioning.
f18.warningLoop: Engine Loops: Ensure your startup/shutdown/idle loops have precise crossfades to avoid popping.

tips.enginePitch: Engines use pitch modulation based on RPM range.
tips.cockpitQuiet: Keep cockpit switches gain around 0.6-0.8 for realism.
tips.explosionsDist: Explosions need large outer_radius (15000+) to be heard from distance.
tips.randomListDesc: Use a list of files for wave to avoid repetitive sound patterns.

types.title: Custom Sound Types & Regex Rules
types.intro: The Asset Library uses Regex (Regular Expressions) to automatically categorize your sounds based on their SDEF file paths. By default, the app can spot Radios, Weapons, Engines, etc. But if you work on a specific mod, you might want to create your own types!
types.regexDesc: A Regex pattern is a sequence of characters that specifies a search pattern in text. When ModMaker scans a file like Effects/Aircrafts/F16/Engine/Burner.sdef, it tests your rules to see which Custom Type to assign.

basic.title: Basic Word Matching
basic.desc: Just type the folder or word you want to find. It is case-insensitive.

or.title: The OR Operator (|)
or.desc: Use the vertical pipe symbol | to say "Match this OR that". Perfect for grouping multiple folders.

strict.title: Strict Matching (^ and $)
strict.desc: Use ^ for the start of the path, and $ for the end.

examples.title: Practical Examples
examples.desc: Common use-cases when creating custom types for DCS.

types.priorityDesc: Custom Rules are evaluated FIRST, top to bottom. If no custom rule matches the SDEF path, the built-in default rules are then used.

share.title: Sharing Types
share.desc: Once you have created types for your mod (e.g., F-14 RIO chatter, F-16 MFD buttons), you can export them. Even better, when you share a Presets file, you can embed your Custom Types into it! When someone imports your preset, ModMaker will automatically install the missing Types and Icons into their library.

docs.title: Documentation
docs.desc: Access built-in guides and references about DCS audio modding.
docs.loadSoundsDesc: When DCS needs a sound, it looks for the SDEF file at the specified path. The SDEF tells DCS:

loadSoundsSteps.2: At what volume to play it (gain)
loadSoundsSteps.3: Where it comes from in 3D space (position, inner_radius, outer_radius)
loadSoundsSteps.4: How to play it (loop, oneshot, random selection)

docs.overridePriority: Override Priority: When you mount your Sounds/ folder using mount_vfs_sound_path, your SDEF files take priority over DCS defaults. You only need to include files you want to change.

examples.engine: Example: Engine Loop with Directional Cone

audio.title: Audio Guide for DCS Sound Modding

f18.title: F/A-18C Complete Sound Reference
f18.desc: Based on the BetterHornet mod — every SDEF file and WAV detailed.

sdeflist.title: Where to find sdef_and_wave_list.txt
sdeflist.desc: The file sdef_and_wave_list.txt contains the full exhaustive list of all sound events and parameters inside DCS World.
sdeflist.tip: You can find it in the installation directory of DCS World. Usually located in your Program Files or Saved Games depending on your setup.

audio.title: Audio Format
audio.tip: DCS supports both .wav and .ogg formats. WAV is higher quality, OGG is smaller.
audio.warning: Browser OGG encoding is limited — WAV conversion is recommended.

action.noAssets: No assets selected — go to the Library to select sounds

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.description: Find answers to common questions about using Better ModMaker.

q1.title: How to update Assets Library?
q1.desc: Go to the Library tab and click 'Reload Library'. The new Scanner modal will appear, allowing you to automatically scan the base game, your CoreMods, and your Saved Games folder.

q2.title: Where is sdef_and_wave_list.txt located?
q2.desc: It is generated by DCS and can be found in your DCS installation folder under \\Doc\\Sounds\\sdef_and_wave_list.txt. Better ModMaker automatically references it if correctly set up.

q3.title: Can you make a mod for another mod?
q3.desc: Yes! The Asset Library recursively loads modded aircraft paths alongside default DCS modules. If your custom mod adds its own sound references, you can find them and override them the normal way.

q4.title: How do I install a generated mod?
q4.desc: Build your mod from the Build tab using OVGME format or a direct folder. Drag the folder into your Saved Games\\DCS\\Mods or use a Mod Manager like OVGME/OpenModManager. For complex instructions, visit

q5.title: How can I participate/contribute or report a bug?
q5.desc: You can contribute via GitHub (Fork the repository and propose Pull Requests) or join the Discord for live feedback and bug reports. Contributions and bug reports are highly appreciated!

q6.title: Are ALL aircraft present in the Library?
q6.desc: Yes, ALL default core aircraft and unencrypted mods are present in the Assets Library. However, finding specific sounds may be long and complex. We recommend using Community Presets to automatically filter sounds for specific actions.

q7.title: How to import modded aircraft SDEFs into the Assets Library?
q7.desc: You no longer need to manually zip and import mods! The built-in Scanner will automatically scan your CoreMods and optionally your Saved Games folder, adding all officially supported and community mods (F-4E, F-14, A-4E-C, etc.) to your Library natively. You can still use the Legacy manual import button via the Scanner Modal if preferred.

q8.title: What does the 'Reload / Rescan' button do?
q8.desc: The Reload button in the Assets Library opens the DCS Scanner modal. It rescans your DCS installation and (optionally) your Saved Games mods folder to rebuild the full asset library from scratch. Use it after installing a new DCS module or mod.

q9.title: What is a Preset?
q9.desc: A Preset saves a snapshot of which assets you have selected in the library — not the SDEF parameter values. It is a shortcut to restore a group of sounds quickly.

q10.title: Can I load an existing mod I already built?
q10.desc: Yes! On the Project page, click Load Existing Mod. You can load from a .zip archive or directly from an unzipped folder on disk (Electron only). The app will import the SDEF files and attempt to match the corresponding audio files automatically.

q11.title: Can I customize how sound types are detected?
q11.desc: Yes. Sound type detection is driven by JSON files in public/sound-types/. The bundled default.json contains all base rules. Add your own .json file with rules (regex → type) and types (label, icon).

q12.title: What are the audio format requirements for DCS?
q12.desc: DCS recommends Mono WAV or OGG files at 44100 Hz or 48000 Hz. Stereo files can be used for non-spatialized cockpit or ambient sounds. The app will warn you if a file is outside recommended specs.

q13.title: My sounds are not playing in-game after installing the mod. Why?
q13.desc: Check: (1) The SDEF wave paths match the actual audio files in Sounds/Effects/. (2) Your entry.lua calls mount_vfs_sound_path. (3) The SDEF path in your mod exactly mirrors the original DCS path.

q14.title: DCS says the Saved Games folder must be clean. Why?
q14.desc: Eagle Dynamics recommends keeping the DCS root installation folder free of third-party mods. All mods should be placed in Saved Games\DCS\Mods\resource\ to avoid conflicts, especially after DCS updates.
